project.name: CrazyCar
project.description: CrazyCar is a networked multiplayer racing game solution with Unity client, Spring Boot server, and Vue admin dashboard. Note: this project has over 200 source files; analysis may take some time.

nodes.name: CrazyCar.cs
nodes.summary: QFramework architecture entry point that registers all systems and models for the Unity client.

nodes.name: NetworkController.cs
nodes.summary: Network communication handler for multiplayer connectivity.

nodes.name: AvatarModel.cs
nodes.summary: Data model defining AvatarInfo, AvatarModel for state management.

nodes.name: EquipModel.cs
nodes.summary: Data model defining EquipInfo, EquipModel for state management.

nodes.name: GameModel.cs
nodes.summary: Data model defining GameModel for state management.

nodes.name: InputModel.cs
nodes.summary: Data model defining InputModel for state management.

nodes.name: MapControllerModel.cs
nodes.summary: Data model defining MapControllerModel for state management.

nodes.name: MatchModel.cs
nodes.summary: Data model defining MatchInfo, MatchRankInfo for state management.

nodes.name: RoomMsgModel.cs
nodes.summary: Data model defining RoomMsgModel for state management.

nodes.name: SettingsModel.cs
nodes.summary: Data model defining SystemSettingsInfo, SettingsModel for state management.

nodes.name: TimeTrialModel.cs
nodes.summary: Data model defining TimeTrialInfo, TimeTrialRankInfo for state management.

nodes.name: UserModel.cs
nodes.summary: Data model defining UserInfo, UserModel for state management.

nodes.name: CrazyCarApplication.java
nodes.summary: Spring Boot main application class for the game server.

layers.name: Unity Client
layers.description: C# game client using QFramework architecture with Model-System-Utility layers

layers.name: Spring Boot Server
layers.description: Java game server with REST APIs and WebSocket handlers

layers.name: Vue Admin Dashboard
layers.description: Vue.js admin panel for game management

tour.title: Project Entry Points
tour.description: CrazyCar has three main entry points: the Unity client (CrazyCar.cs), Spring Boot server (CrazyCarApplication.java), and Vue admin dashboard (main.js).

tour.title: QFramework Architecture - Model Layer
tour.description: The client uses QFramework with a strict Model-System-Utility architecture. Models manage game state like player data, match info, and equipment.

tour.title: QFramework Architecture - System Layer
tour.description: Systems contain game logic for networking, player management, time trials, and matchmaking.

tour.title: Network Communication
tour.description: Dual-protocol networking using both KCP (low-latency UDP) and WebSocket for real-time multiplayer synchronization.
